citations.claim: The CLI imports CryptoKit for cryptographic operations, EigenInferenceEnclave for core functionality, and Foundation for runtime support
The CLI provides two main commands: attest for generating signed attestation blobs and info for showing Secure Enclave availability, with emphasis on ephemeral keys
The cmdAttest function validates Secure Enclave availability, creates ephemeral identity, and uses AttestationService to generate signed attestations
Secure Enclave availability is checked before attempting cryptographic operations, with error exit if unavailable
The cmdInfo function reports device capabilities including secure enclave availability and ephemeral public key
Command-line argument parsing handles attest command with optional encryption-key and binary-hash parameters
Usage documentation shows command structure and options for both attest and info commands
JSON encoding uses ISO 8601 dates and sorted keys for deterministic output to STDOUT
Error handling outputs error messages to STDERR and exits with code 1 on failures
WebSocket bridge functionality was removed due to Apple keychain restrictions with ACME managed identities
Package requires macOS 13.0 or later for Secure Enclave functionality
CLI executable target depends directly on EigenInferenceEnclave library
Device info includes secure_enclave_available boolean and key_persistence set to ephemeral
